description_long: This is a handwoven, round basket-style bowl made from thick, natural-colored fibers, likely straw or rattan. The bowl features a tightly braided pattern throughout its structure, giving it a rustic and artisanal appearance. Two sturdy, looped handles are integrated into the rim on opposite sides, allowing for easy carrying. The interior is spacious and open, with the same braided texture as the exterior, and the base is slightly raised in the center, forming a subtle spiral. The color is a consistent light tan, typical of untreated plant fibers. There are no visible decorative patterns or embellishments, emphasizing its utilitarian and organic design.
description: A round, handwoven straw bowl with two looped handles and a braided texture, finished in a natural tan color.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the tightly braided, circular pattern of the bowl's base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the bowl's depth and one of the looped handles.
description_view_2: Interior view displaying the open, spacious cavity and both handles on the rim.
description_view_3: Profile view emphasizing the bowl's rounded shape and the symmetry of the handles.
attribute_context_of_use: serving or holding food items such as bread, fruit, or snacks

license_info.creator_display_name: Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East
